subject: English
skill: Central Idea, Text Structure, and Source Integration

lesson.story_mission: Enter Source Synthesis Station, where readers and writers solve challenges with careful reasoning.
lesson.objective: I can central idea, text structure, and source integration using Grade 6 English strategies.
lesson.concept_explanation: Central Idea, Text Structure, and Source Integration means reading or writing with a clear purpose, accurate details, and evidence that proves the idea.
lesson.common_misconception: A learner may choose a detail that is interesting but does not prove the answer, or write an answer without enough explanation.
lesson.real_world_connection: Middle school readers and writers use this skill in novels, articles, research projects, discussions, and essays.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the whole text or prompt, identify the important idea, choose relevant evidence, and explain how the evidence proves your thinking.
lesson.mini_lesson: Step 1: read closely. Step 2: name the idea or claim. Step 3: select reasons and evidence. Step 4: explain the connection in your own words.

worked_examples.prompt: Watch: Integrate details from two sources.
worked_examples.title: Ancient Canals
worked_examples.text: Farmers in dry regions built canals to move water from rivers to fields. The canals required planning because too much water could damage crops. Communities worked together to clean and repair the channels.
worked_examples.sentence: Farmers in dry regions built canals to move water from rivers to fields. The canals required planning because too much water could damage crops. Communities worked together to clean and repair the channels.
worked_examples.main_idea: canals helped farmers manage scarce water
worked_examples.correct_answer: Both sources show that people use planning to improve environment and learning.
worked_examples.answer: Both sources show that people use planning to improve environment and learning.
worked_examples.explanation: The answer is supported by details about ancient canals.

source_a.title: Ancient Canals
source_a.text: Farmers in dry regions built canals to move water from rivers to fields. The canals required planning because too much water could damage crops. Communities worked together to clean and repair the channels.

source_b.title: School Gardens
source_b.text: A school garden can become an outdoor classroom. Students measure plant growth, study insects, and harvest herbs for the cafeteria. The garden needs a schedule so plants are watered on weekends.

worked_examples.synthesis: Both sources show that people use planning to improve environment and learning.
choices: Both sources show that people use planning to improve environment and learning.
Ancient Canals is the only useful source.

misconception_bank.topic_central_idea_confusion: Names only the broad topic instead of what the author says about it.
misconception_bank.summary_opinion_error: Adds personal opinion or a minor detail to an objective summary.
misconception_bank.text_structure_confusion: Misses signal words or how ideas are organized.
misconception_bank.source_integration_error: Uses only one source or combines details inaccurately.
